item.title: Base element
item.description: Let’s start with the MDN docs for the base element, they say: The HTML <base> element specifies the base URL to use for all relative URLs contained within a document. That sounds like it could be pretty useful. You could change the base to be the root, so there would be no more getting relative/absolute URLs mixed up. <head> <base href="/"> </head> <a href="test">Relative URL</a> <a href="/test">Absolute URL</a> <a href="https://google.

item.title: Gnocchi with Chicken & Tomatoes
item.description: Sometimes when you’re in a rush it can be tempting to eat lots of snacks, or just a bowl of cereal (not that there is anything wrong with cereal). Having a few quick to make recipes to hand is always a good thing and you’ll feel less guilty than eating just junk food. This recipe will serve 2-4 people, depending on people’s appetites. Ingredients 250g chicken breast 450g gnocchi 300g cherry tomatoes 50g fresh basil Prep Cut the chicken into chunks, about 2cm each Cut the tomatoes in half Tear the basil into small pieces Instructions Cook the gnocchi according to package directions.

item.title: Anatomy of a CSS rule
item.description: I was describing the mark-up of a CSS rule to someone learning CSS. I though it would be handy to have a reference for it, so I created the following reference image for him. Lets break it down into parts: The whole thing is a ruleset The curly braces and everything inside is a declaration block The bit before the opening curly brace is a selector A declaration has two parts, separated by a colon and ending in a semicolon Each declaration has a property (before the semi colon) and a value (after the semi colon) A selector can be any, or a combination of:

item.title: Open external links in a new window
item.description: Update 11/04/2018: I wrote this a long time ago, since then I’ve stopped using jQuery. I’m not saying that this is a good thing, in fact, it’s probably a bad thing. A user should always have the choice on if a link should open in a new window or not. However, this is a small bit of javascript that will make all external links open in a new tab/window.

item.title: Pixel escape website
item.description: Update 10/02/2017: Unfortunatly the site is no more and the game will no longer be updated. Dont expect the links below to work. The game is about saving Pixel-land, the world where innocent little pixels live. You do this by using the space key to jump and the mouse can draw lines for the pixels to walk on. You can view a trailer for the game or play it. The site has been styled using assets from the game.

item.title: How easy are you to find online?
item.description: Not a stalker, honest! What I am about to say does seem very stalker like, it is not intended to be. It is intended to show the amount of data we openly share online, our personal lives left wide open, which some people would be more than happy to exploit. For the sake of anonymity and online security of the people I am talking about, I have chosen to omit names, places and any other identifiable info (including pronouns, the people will be referred to as ‘they’ where appropriate).

item.title: Monzo
item.description: What is monzo? Monzo is an online bank. But unlike all the old banks, it’s built on modern technology. This allows them to do amazing things like give you instant notification of payments. My experience I have used Monzo for a long time. So long that I was with them when they were called Mondo, and only offered a pre-paid card that needed topping up. Unlike today, where they are a fully fledge bank.
